visual: Repeat of move 9 — wide bow stance, hooked rear hand, front palm pushing outward.
motion_description: The movement begins with the rear arm extended horizontally to form a static hook at shoulder height, while the front hand is initially held near the opposite shoulder. Maintaining a slow, deliberate tempo, the practitioner rotates their torso approximately 90 degrees forward as the front foot steps outward to establish a wide bow stance. The weight then smoothly shifts onto the bending front leg while the front arm extends outward, concluding with a steady forward palm push at chest level.
